mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present equity values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return following low FEDFUNDS observations is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above recent lows signals deteriorating labor demand, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate trigger fires.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and industrial production indicates aggregate demand is outpacing productive capacity, driving margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and INDPRO fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term contributes no incremental predictive power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1%, month-over-month payroll employment increases signal that accommodative policy is successfully transmitting to the real economy, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1%, month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1500 thousand units reflect strong consumer confidence and durable investment demand, feeding into broad economic expansion and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is negative more than 50% of the time over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index acceleration above 3% signals impending monetary tightening that raises real discount rates and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when the CPI acceleration trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in unemployment alongside rising payroll employment tightens labor markets, boosts aggregate wage income and consumption expenditure, and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint movement of falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS does not correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (Spearman correlation < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production index reflects rising manufacturing throughput and capacity utilization, which supports corporate operating leverage and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly increases in INDPRO are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.
